title: Multiple variables should not be declared on the same line
description: Multiple variables should not be declared on the same lineAnalyze your codeconsistency-formattedmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEconventionbased-on-misracertWhy is this an issue?More InfoDeclaring multiple variables or members on the same line hinders readability. Moreover, as soon as they contain references, pointers, or assignments, they become confusing for maintainers.This rule raises an issue when a declaration declares multiple variables or members.int i1, j1; // Noncompliant int i2, *j2; // Noncompliant int *i3, &j3 = i2; // NoncompliantGiving each declaration its own line makes the code more maintainable.int i1; int j1; int i2; int *j2; int *i3; int &j3 = i2;Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: int i1, j1; // Noncompliant: multiple variables on same line
int i2, *j2; // Noncompliant: confusing with pointer
char *ptr1, ptr2; // Noncompliant: only ptr1 is pointer
compliant:
int i1;
int j1; // Compliant: separate declarations

int i2;
int *j2; // Compliant: clear pointer declaration

char *ptr1;
char *ptr2; // Compliant: both clearly pointers

checks.message: Multiple variables should not be declared on the same line. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
